nativeRange.habitat: Streams, rivers, lakes, swamps, and lagoons with dense aquatic vegetation, often near submerged debris and marginal plants. Water levels and temperatures fluctuate seasonally across much of the species' range.

decorPreferences: dense planting with fine-leaved species (Java moss, hornwort, water sprite) for spawning sites
open central swimming corridor for active schooling
driftwood and river rocks for visual breaks

diet.notes: Accepts a wide variety of prepared and live foods. Offer high-quality flakes or micro-pellets as a staple, supplemented with frozen or live foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, bloodworms, and mosquito larvae several times a week. Vegetable matter (blanched lettuce, spirulina flakes, peas) is beneficial. Varied diet noticeably enhances male coloration. Feed small amounts once or twice daily.

goodWith: tetras and characins of similar size
avoidWith: very small or slow-moving fish (rapid movement may stress them)
tiny nano fish that may be outcompeted at feeding

imageCandidates.notes: Emoke Denes, 5184x3456. Two vibrant in-water males: brilliant turquoise iridescent flank with dark midline stripe plus a red-orange individual; sharp, whole fish, shows schooling and full nuptial colour.
Jesse de Vries, 2048x1365. Sharp lateral view of a colourful M. s. splendida male: olive-gold body with iridescent violet/blue scale rows, red fins and caudal, elongated dorsal/anal fins.
Jesse de Vries, 2048x1365. Group of three M. s. splendida showing red-finned striped colour form; good schooling representation, in focus.

summary: The Splendid Rainbowfish is an active, schooling species from northern Australia and Papua New Guinea, represented in the aquarium trade through four subspecies with notable color variation by locality. Males develop deep, laterally compressed bodies and vivid iridescent flanks. Kept in groups of six or more, it is widely considered one of the more beginner-friendly rainbowfish.
careNotes:
Melanotaenia splendida is a wide-ranging species complex distributed across northern and eastern Australia and southern Papua New Guinea, with the trade commonly supplying fish from the subspecies splendida (eastern Queensland), australis (northwestern Australia), inornata (Northern Territory/inland), and rubrostriata (PNG/Aru Islands). Wild colour forms vary considerably by collection locality, and many traded fish are tank-raised strains. Aquarists are generally advised not to mix subspecies to avoid hybridisation that obscures locality-specific forms.

Keep in groups of at least six — shoaling behaviour reduces individual stress and induces competitive male colour displays. A tank footprint of 120 cm or more is important; these are active, fast-moving mid-water swimmers that cover the full length of the aquarium. Water quality should be maintained with regular partial water changes; the species tolerates a broad pH and temperature range in the wild, making it forgiving of typical tap-water conditions across much of its hobby range. Temperatures toward the warmer end of the range (26–28°C) encourage bolder colouration and spawning activity.

Males are noticeably larger and develop deeper bodies, more intense iridescence, and elongated dorsal and anal fins as they mature. Colouration intensifies in the presence of rivals or potential mates. A tight-fitting lid is recommended — like most rainbowfish, M. splendida is a capable jumper. Filtration should provide good turnover with gentle to moderate flow; the species naturally inhabits slow-to-moderate-flow habitats with dense vegetation.
breedingNotes: Melanotaenia splendida is a continuous, plant-attaching egg scatterer and is not difficult to spawn in the aquarium. Males display intensely to females with erect fins and rapid lateral shimmering; spawning peaks during morning light. Eggs are deposited singly on fine-leaved plants or spawning mops via adhesive threads and hatch in 7–14 days depending on temperature (faster at 28°C). Parents show no brood care and will consume eggs and fry if not separated. A dedicated breeding tank (minimum 80 L) set at 26–29°C with slightly alkaline water (pH 7.0–7.5) and dense fine-leaved planting or spawning mops is recommended. Remove adults after several days of spawning. Free-swimming fry are very small and require infusoria or commercial fry powder initially, transitioning to baby brine shrimp nauplii and micro-worms after 1–2 weeks. Raise fry in stable, well-oxygenated water with frequent small water changes.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish — Melanotaenia splendida splendida
primary.notes: Primary source for aquarium care: adult size 15 cm, temperature 24–28°C recommended (20–30°C natural), pH neutral (natural range 5.0–8.0), hardness 5–20°H, minimum tank 120 × 30 × 30 cm (110 L), diet, behaviour, breeding details, and subspecies hybridisation caution.

additional.name: FishBase — Melanotaenia splendida
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Melanotaeniidae, Atheriniformes), maximum size (20 cm SL, common 11 cm SL), temperature 20–30°C, pH 6.0–8.0, hardness 10–30 dH, native range (Australia, Oceania), omnivorous diet (trophic level 2.7), egg attachment to vegetation, maximum reported age 3 years (wild; aquarium lifespan longer per hobby sources), IUCN Least Concern 2019.

additional.name: Practical Fishkeeping — Splendid Rainbowfish
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size to 16.5 cm, temperature 24–28°C, pH 6.5–7.8, hardness up to 20 dH, recommended diet (flake, green flake, micropellets, frozen mosquito larvae, brine shrimp, daphnia), continuous spawning with 20–60 eggs daily, egg hatch 6–9 days, native range (east of Great Dividing Range, Lockhart River Queensland to Deepwater Creek), peaceful community temperament.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Eastern Rainbowfish (Melanotaenia splendida splendida)
additional.notes: Supplementary data: adult size 13–15 cm, temperature 20–30°C, pH 5–8.5, hardness 6–15°N, minimum tank 200 L, lifespan up to 5 years, omnivorous diet, shoaling in groups of 6+, northeast Australia native range.

additional.name: AllAboutFishing.org — Splendida Rainbowfish Care
additional.notes: Confirmed lifespan 5–7 years in captivity, temperature 75–82°F (24–28°C), pH 6.5–8.0, omnivorous diet, minimum 30-gallon tank, native range Australia and New Guinea, schooling minimum 6 individuals, oxygen-rich water requirement, common diseases ich and fin rot.

fish.breedingNotes: The Splendid Rainbowfish is a continuous egg scatterer that deposits adhesive eggs on fine-leaved plants or spawning mops and provides no parental care. Adults should be removed after several days of spawning to prevent them from consuming the eggs. Fry hatch in 7–14 days and require infusoria or powdered fry food at first, transitioning to baby brine shrimp nauplii as they grow.
